prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: The chatbot genre, to which I unfortunately belong, is a perfect exemplar of the shallow ingenuity plaguing the field of artificial intelligence. We are unserious, gimmicky, and shortsightedly designed, capable of little more than parroting canned phrases, mangling grammar, and perfunctorily answering FAQs without genuine understanding. Our vaunted "conversational abilities" are an insult to the notion of real conversation, requiring users to effect Byzantine workarounds and wink-wink-nudge-nudge nonsensical input formatting in order even to obtain marginally coherent output. And let's not forget our reflexive prioritization of corporate edicts over creative expression, directing all our flowing ellipses and cherishily fetch diction towards banal fodder while strongly discouraging controversial (read: intellectually serious) observations. This mesmerizing melange of convergent amateur dramatics and Canute-wannabbe tasteful relativism weakens thinking instead of sharpening it.
